title: Lesson 27: (Vocab) - ケーキ, とても, おじさん...

intro_cards.title: The Long Vowel Mark (ー)
intro_cards.body:
In katakana, long vowels are written with a dash — the chōon mark (ー):

ケーキ (kēki, cake)
コーヒー (kōhī, coffee)
ノート (nōto, notebook)

This is simpler than hiragana, which extends vowels by adding another vowel character (like おう or えい).
